Executive Summary

Key Takeaways

  • Top performing stores: House of Innovation NYC (66.0), Battery Park (59.3), Atlantic Center (55.8) lead the portfolio on overall store strength. These locations benefit from strong trade area vibrancy, high athletic ecosystem density, and deep event amplification signals — with major sportswear rivals operating within 0.5 miles of each. The spring and summer calendar is particularly strong for these stores, anchored by NYC Pride March (June 28) passing directly past HOI, the TD Five Boro Bike Tour starting 0.3 miles from Battery Park, and Governors Ball at Flushing Meadows supporting the Queens clearance store.
  • Key seasonal demand windows across the portfolio: Mother's Day (May 10), Memorial Day weekend (May 23–25), and Father's Day (June 21) represent the three highest-volume retail weekends of the spring calendar and apply across all nine stores. Good Friday (April 3) and Juneteenth (June 19) add further demand spikes, with Juneteenth carrying particular significance for Harlem, East New York, and Atlantic Center given their community positioning.
  • Event intelligence covers confirmed events through June 2026, including running events with strong Nike-demographic overlap: the NYCRUNS Brooklyn Experience Half Marathon (April 26, 25,000 runners through North Brooklyn), the RBC Brooklyn Half Marathon (May 16, 25,000 runners from Prospect Park), and the TD Five Boro Bike Tour (May 3, 32,000 cyclists). Governors Ball at Flushing Meadows (June 5–7, 60,000+ daily attendees headlined by A$AP Rocky, Stray Kids, and Lorde) is the highest-impact June event for the Flushing Queens store.
  • Champs Sports (East New York), JD Sports (Harlem), and Nordstrom Rack (Staten Island) represent the highest localised competitive pressure by proximity and promotional intensity. Direct athletic and footwear competitors — including Adidas, New Balance, Reebok, Under Armour, Skechers, Foot Locker, and SNIPES — are the primary revenue deflection risks across the portfolio, with threat levels varying by store based on distance and offer strength.
  • Three stores are structurally underperforming versus their trade area opportunity: Factory Store – Staten Island (+8.5), Unite – East New York (+5.7), and Atlantic Center (+5.6) all show Net Opportunity materially above Overall Strength — indicating untapped potential in high-transit, mixed-use neighbourhoods where current trade area conversion is below what the footfall environment would support. Loyalty activation, community programming, and event-led traffic strategies are the highest-ROI levers for these locations.
  • Unite – East New York (20.0) is a mall-anchored, community-driven store where demand is generated primarily through neighbourhood loyalty and holiday retail weekends rather than external event amplification. Mother's Day, Father's Day, Memorial Day, and Juneteenth are the four highest-impact demand signals for this location. The low dining density (8.2), walkability (16.1), and transit connectivity (25.6) confirm that event-led foot traffic strategies will have limited incremental impact compared to in-mall loyalty and community activation.
  • Lowest-scoring stores: Unite – East New York (20.0) and Nike – North Bronx (18.1) reflect low-density outer-borough trade areas with limited anchor ecosystems and minimal tourism draw. Both show Event Amplification scores below 14, confirming that external event uplift will have structurally limited impact relative to the rest of the portfolio. For North Bronx, the NYBG Orchid Show (through April 26, 2.3 miles) and Bronx Week (May 17–18) represent the most credible near-term foot traffic signals.

Store Context
Nike House of Innovation NYC is a Nike retail location in Manhattan, NYC — serving a diverse mix of performance athletes, streetwear enthusiasts, and casual shoppers. The store benefits from Nike's premium positioning and strong brand loyalty within its local trade area.
★ Top Event
Easter Parade & Bonnet Festival
2026-04-05 • 5th Ave / 49th St → 5th Ave / 57th St • 0.1 mi from store • Parade
Impact: 10/10 High Incrementality
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Easter Parade & Bonnet Festival is expected to draw a very high foot traffic impact due to its major crowd draw status and proximity to the store.
NYC Pride March
2026-06-28 • 5th Ave / 25th St → 7th Ave / 15th St • 0.2 mi from store • Parade
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The NYC Pride March routes directly along 5th Avenue past the store, drawing an estimated 4 million spectators. This is the largest LGBTQ+ parade in the world and one of the highest foot-traffic days of the year for Midtown retail. Weekend-long Pride events saturate the surrounding blocks, creating sustained demand across Saturday and Sunday.

Memorial Day Weekend
2026-05-23 to 2026-05-25 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Memorial Day weekend marks the unofficial start of summer and is one of the strongest retail weekends of the year. Midtown sees elevated tourist and local foot traffic across the full three-day weekend, with strong gifting and seasonal apparel purchasing. Nike performs well during this period as consumers refresh athletic wardrobes ahead of summer.

DSW: Ongoing Free Shipping on Orders $75+ or for VIP Members
Free shipping on orders $75+ or free delivery for VIP members (Ongoing)
This promotion offers free shipping when customers spend $75 or more, which encourages bigger orders but may slow down some shoppers who don’t want to meet that minimum. Because it’s always available online and close to your store, it gently nudges customers to shop more without causing much hassle. Overall, it helps increase sales but isn’t a strong driver on. Monitor this promotion for its impact on customer traffic to nearby Nike locations.

Store Context
Nike Battery Park is a Nike retail location in Manhattan, NYC — serving a diverse mix of performance athletes, streetwear enthusiasts, and casual shoppers. The store benefits from Nike's premium positioning and strong brand loyalty within its local trade area.
★ Top Event
TD Five Boro Bike Tour
2026-05-03 • Franklin St & Church St (start) → all five boroughs • 0.3 mi from store • Sports
Impact: 9.0/10 High Incrementality
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Five Boro Bike Tour starts at Franklin St and Church St, less than 0.3 miles from Battery Park. Over 32,000 cyclists from around the world converge on Lower Manhattan from early morning, with staging and wave releases running until 10:30 am. The surrounding streets see massive pre-ride foot traffic, and the athletic demographic is one of the strongest overlaps with the Nike customer. This is a top-tier demand event for this store.
LES Print Fair
2026-04-25 to 2026-04-26 • Sara D. Roosevelt Park • 1.3 mi from store • Street Fair
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The LES Print Fair is expected to draw a strong crowd with an estimated turnout between 300 and 1,800 attendees, which is well below the venue capacity of about 5,000 people. Despite the event being 1.3 miles from the store and the uncertainty in actual attendance, the weekend timing and strong crowd draw contribute to a high impact on foot traffic.
Mott Street Block Party
2026-04-18 • Mott Street • 0.9 mi from store • Street Fair
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Mott Street Block Party is expected to draw a steady crowd of approximately 120 to 700 attendees within a venue capacity of about 2,000 people, which indicates a high impact on foot traffic due to its weekend timing and close proximity to the Nike Battery Park store, though actual turnout may be significantly lower than capacity.
LES Little League Opening Day Parade
2026-04-04 • Parade starts at Grand Street Settlement • 1.8 mi from store • Parade
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The event is expected to draw a small crowd of approximately 30 to 175 attendees, which is well below the venue capacity of about 500 people, indicating limited but noticeable foot traffic impact; given the event's distance of 1.8 miles from the store and weekend morning timing, demand is likely moderate but not maximal.
Smorgasburg at the World Trade Center
2026-04-03 to 2026-04-24 • World Trade Center Plaza • 0.1 mi from store • Food Event
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The event is expected to draw a strong crowd with an attendance likely between 300 and 1,800 people, which is significant but well below the venue capacity of about 5,000. Its very close proximity to the store and timing around lunchtime on a weekday contribute to a high impact on foot traffic, though actual turnout may vary.
Brooklyn Eats Food Festival (Nearby)
2026-04-25 to 2026-04-26 • Brooklyn Bridge Park (Nearby) • 1.4 mi from store • Food Event
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Brooklyn Eats Food Festival is expected to draw a high level of foot traffic due to its major crowd draw status and weekend timing, with an estimated turnout between 600 and 3,500 attendees, which is well below the venue capacity of about 10,000 people; despite the event being 1.4 miles from the store, this proximity combined with the event's popularity supports a high impact on demand.
Korea Festival
2026-04-25 to 2026-04-26 • Koreatown (32nd Street) • 2.9 mi from store • Festival
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Korea Festival is expected to draw a very high foot traffic impact due to its major crowd draw status and weekend timing; despite being nearly 3 miles from the store, the large expected attendance supports a very high demand impact.
New York Knicks vs. Boston Celtics
2026-04-09 • Madison Square Garden • 2.8 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The event at Madison Square Garden has a high impact on foot traffic due to its large venue capacity of about 20,000 and an expected attendance range of 8,800 to 19,600, indicating a major crowd draw despite being 2.8 miles from the store and occurring on a weekday evening.
New York Knicks vs. Toronto Raptors
2026-04-10 • Madison Square Garden • 2.8 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The event at Madison Square Garden has a high impact on foot traffic because it is a major crowd draw with an expected attendance between 8,800 and 19,600 people, approaching the venue's 20,000 capacity. Although the venue is about 2.8 miles from the store and the event occurs on a weekday evening, the strong attendance intent from ticketed sales supports a likely significant increase in nearby foot traffic.
Memorial Day Weekend
2026-05-23 to 2026-05-25 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Memorial Day weekend drives elevated foot traffic in Lower Manhattan and Battery Park as tourists and local residents take advantage of the long weekend. Retail spending across the FiDi area increases materially over the three-day period.
Mother's Day
2026-05-10 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Mother's Day drives elevated gifting purchases in the Battery Park / FiDi area. Weekend foot traffic is meaningfully higher than a typical Sunday, with strong demand for athletic apparel and footwear as gift categories.
Father's Day
2026-06-21 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Father's Day drives gifting foot traffic across Lower Manhattan. Athletic footwear and apparel are top-performing gift categories, and the FiDi area sees elevated weekend retail traffic.
Good Friday
2026-04-03 • Citywide — NYC schools closed • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Good Friday school closures drive elevated daytime foot traffic across Lower Manhattan. The Easter long weekend creates a two-day retail opportunity, with Friday and Sunday both seeing above-average consumer activity.
NYC Pride March
2026-06-28 • 5th Ave / 25th St → Greenwich Village • 2.5 mi from store • Parade
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
NYC Pride March draws millions of participants and spectators across Manhattan. Pride weekend significantly elevates foot traffic across Lower Manhattan and FiDi as visitors extend their stay. The Battery Park waterfront area sees strong ancillary crowd flow on Pride weekend.

Active Promotions Detail
Converse • 1.50 mi 3.4 · Low
2026-03-27
Converse: Free standard shipping for members ongoing
Free standard shipping on all orders for Converse members; non-members get free shipping on orders $75+; guest orders under $75 pay $8 standard shipping (ongoing)
This promotion offers free standard shipping for members, which makes it easier for them to complete purchases without extra cost. However, since the discount doesn’t apply directly to the products customers are buying and non-members still face shipping fees unless they spend $75, the overall boost in sales is modest. Keep encouraging customers to join the membership to get the best shipping benefit and reduce their buying hesitation.
New Balance • 3.22 mi 3.2 · Low
2026-03-27
New Balance: Free Shipping on Orders Over $50 for Guests (March 2026)
Free standard shipping for guests on orders over $50; members always get free shipping (expires ongoing)
This promotion offers free shipping on orders over $50 instead of the usual $75, which can encourage customers to add more to their cart to save on shipping. However, because the items promoted don’t closely match what customers usually buy together, and the offer mainly targets online shoppers who must meet a spending threshold, the overall boost in sales is modest. Keep highlighting the savings clearly to help customers see the value and reduce hesitation.
Converse • 1.50 mi 3.1 · Low
2026-03-27
Converse: Up to 65% off select styles seasonal sale ongoing
Up to 65% off select styles during seasonal sales, including 50% off specially marked items and 25% off new arrivals (ongoing in 2026)
This promotion offers a clear discount on select styles, making it easy for customers to understand and use. Because it happens regularly during the season and is always available, shoppers know when to expect it and can plan their visits. However, since the discount isn’t very strong and the styles don’t closely match popular competitors, it may not drive a big increase in sales on its own. Keep highlighting the ease and timing to encourage customers to take advantage.

Store Context
Nike Atlantic Center is a Nike retail location in Brooklyn, NYC — serving a diverse mix of performance athletes, streetwear enthusiasts, and casual shoppers. The store benefits from Nike's premium positioning and strong brand loyalty within its local trade area.
★ Top Event
UFC Fight Night
2026-04-18 • Barclays Center • 0.1 mi from store • Sports
Impact: 9.5/10 High Incrementality
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The UFC Fight Night at Barclays Center, with a venue capacity of about 17.7k and an expected turnout between 7.1k and 16k attendees, is a very high impact event due to its major crowd draw and very close proximity (0.1 miles) to the Nike Atlantic Center store, especially occurring on a weekend evening which typically increases foot traffic; however, actual turnout may be significantly lower than capacity.

2026-04-03 • Barclays Center • 0.1 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Brooklyn Nets vs. Atlanta Hawks game at Barclays Center, with a venue capacity of about 17.7k and expected attendance between 7.8k and 17.4k, is a very high impact event due to its major crowd draw very close to the Nike Atlantic Center store, likely generating significant foot traffic despite some uncertainty in actual turnout.

2026-05-16 • Prospect Park → Coney Island Boardwalk • 1.7 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The RBC Brooklyn Half Marathon is the largest half marathon in the United States, with approximately 25,000 runners departing from Prospect Park. The course routes along Ocean Parkway toward Coney Island, but the pre-race staging area and post-race celebrations near Prospect Park draw significant foot traffic close to Atlantic Center on race morning. The athletic consumer demographic aligns strongly with Nike.

2026-05-23 to 2026-05-25 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Memorial Day weekend is a top retail weekend for mall-adjacent and value stores. Atlantic Center benefits from strong Brooklyn family shopping traffic as residents take advantage of the long weekend. Seasonal athletic and footwear purchasing is elevated.

Active Promotions Detail
Skechers • 0.41 mi 3.7 · Low
2026-03-04
Skechers: Buy One, Get One 50% Off on Regular Priced Styles Online Only
Current promotion (2026-03-04 to 2026-04-06): Buy One, Get One 50% Off on regular priced styles online only.
This promotion offers a straightforward Buy One, Get One 50% Off deal online, making it easy for customers to take advantage without extra steps. However, because the discounted items don’t closely match popular products in your store, fewer shoppers may be drawn in by this offer. Still, its timing and convenience help keep interest moderate, so encouraging customers to explore these styles could improve results.
Soccer Post Brooklyn • 0.65 mi 3.4 · Low
2026-03-01
Soccer Post Brooklyn: Free shipping promotion with redeemable code March 2026
Free shipping promotion with redeemable code (March 2026)
This free shipping promo is easy for customers to use since they just enter a code at checkout, and it runs all month long, giving plenty of chances to take advantage. However, because the promoted item is quite different from what customers usually buy, and the promo source isn’t very familiar, it’s not driving a strong boost in sales. Still, being close to the store helps a bit, so reminding nearby shoppers about the free shipping could improve results.
SNIPES • 0.62 mi 3.3 · Low
2026-03-27
SNIPES: End of Season Sale up to 70% off select styles ongoing
End of Season Sale: Up to 70% off select styles (Ongoing) [Groupon]
The discount is strong and easy to redeem, but the limited assortment depth compared to Nike means deflection risk is moderate rather than high. Price-sensitive streetwear buyers represent the primary at-risk segment.
DSW • 0.57 mi 3.0 · Low
2026-03-27
DSW: Ongoing $10 off $49 purchase and BOGO promotions
$10 off $49 purchase, Buy One Get One Free promotions available (expires ongoing) [Source] - https://www.dsw.com/content/coupons-and-promo-codes
This promotion has a modest impact on customer buying behavior. While the buy-one-get-one offer is appealing, the need to be a VIP member and enter a promo code adds some steps that might slow customers down. Because the deal runs regularly and is easy to find nearby, it still encourages steady interest, but it won’t cause a big spike in sales.
Brooklyn Fanatics • 0.70 mi 2.9 · Low
2026-03-27
Brooklyn Fanatics: FanCash rewards program offering daily promos
FanCash rewards program: Earn FanCash based on bet odds (0.01% to 10% of bet amount), redeemable for bonus bets or official merchandise; Daily promos include NBA profit boosts, FanCash Drops, and Fanatics FanCash Draws for prizes and tickets; No Sweat Bet tokens available to get bonus bets back if wager loses
The promotion targets categories that do not closely align with what most Nike customers purchase, so the overall competitive impact is somewhat limited. Monitor for crossover appeal among value-focused footwear shoppers.
Brooklyn Fanatics • 0.70 mi 2.8 · Low
2026-03-27
Brooklyn Fanatics: 100% Profit Boost Token daily for 5 days
Receive one 100% Profit Boost Token per day for 5 days (max $25 stake, min odds -500, tokens expire 24 hours after issuance, opt-in required)
This promotion gives customers a free daily profit boost token for five days, which encourages them to come back regularly. Because the tokens expire quickly and require opting in, some customers might find it a bit tricky to use. Also, the items involved aren’t a perfect match to what customers usually buy, so it may not drive a lot of extra sales. However, since your store is close by, nearby shoppers are more likely to take advantage of it.
Brooklyn Fanatics • 0.70 mi 2.8 · Low
2026-03-27
Brooklyn Fanatics: Bet $1+ daily for up to $250 FanCash (ongoing)
Bet $1+ on any market; if the bet loses, receive up to $25 in FanCash daily for 10 days, totaling up to $250 in FanCash (new users, opt-in required, expires 7 days after issuance)
This promotion encourages customers to place a small daily bet for 10 days, rewarding them with FanCash if they lose. Because the bet is low-cost but requires daily participation and opting in, some customers might hesitate to join. However, the FanCash expires quickly, motivating timely use, and since your store is nearby, customers are more likely to redeem their rewards here. Overall, expect moderate interest with a good chance of repeat visits during the promotion.
Brooklyn Fanatics • 0.70 mi 2.7 · Low
2026-03-27

Brooklyn Nets merchandise clearance sale ongoing at Fanatics.com (no specific expiration or pricing details)
This Brooklyn Nets clearance sale offers a big price drop that’s easy for customers to use, which helps attract shoppers nearby. However, since the sale items don’t closely match what customers usually buy, it may not drive a lot of extra purchases. Still, because the promotion runs regularly and is simple to redeem, it can steadily bring in interested fans looking for a good deal.
adidas • 3.37 mi 2.7 · Low
2026-03-27
adidas: Adidas Promo Code offer valid through April 3, 2026
Adidas Promo Code offer valid through April 3, 2026 (expires 2026-04-03)
This promotion offers a modest discount that’s easy for customers to use online, which helps encourage some purchases. However, because the discount isn’t very strong and the products don’t closely match popular competitor items, it won’t drive a big sales boost. Still, since the offer lasts for a while and comes from a trusted source, it can steadily attract interested shoppers over time.
SNIPES • 0.62 mi 2.6 · Low
2026-03-27
SNIPES: Clearance items up to 69% off and sale section up to 71% off ongoing
Clearance items up to 69% off, Sale section up to 71% off (Ongoing) [Savings.com]
The easy savings and frequent promotional updates attract shoppers who prioritise value over brand loyalty. This represents a moderate ongoing deflection risk, particularly for performance-casual footwear buyers.
SNIPES • 0.62 mi 2.6 · Low
2026-03-27
SNIPES: App-exclusive promo codes and early access to flash sales ongoing
App-exclusive promo codes and early access to flash sales via Snipes app (Ongoing) [Groupon]
This promotion offers a strong discount and is easy for customers to redeem once they have the app, but because they need to download and use the app first, some may hesitate. Since the deal is ongoing and nearby, it encourages repeat visits and quick decisions. However, the limited overlap with popular Nike products means it might not attract all sneaker shoppers. Overall, it’s a good way to bring in loyal app users but may have less impact on casual buyers.
Reebok • 4.20 mi 2.6 · Low
2026-03-27
Reebok: Ongoing Free Standard Shipping On US Orders Over $75; All Orders Free Shipping for Authentic Members
Free standard shipping on US orders over $75; Authentic Membership subscribers get free shipping on all orders regardless of size (ongoing)
The ongoing nature of the offer can gently encourage customers to add more items to reach the discount threshold, increasing basket size for Reebok while posing a modest deflection risk for Nike's entry-level performance range.
Adidas Originals • 3.37 mi 2.6 · Low
2026-03-20
Adidas Originals: Spring Sale extra 30% off full-priced items online and in-store until 2026-03-30
Spring Sale extra 30% off full-priced items online and in-store (valid 2026-03-20 00:01 PST through 2026-03-30 23:59 PST) [Adidas.com]
Expect a modest but measurable lift in customer interest during the promotional window. The strong brand overlap and proximity make this one of the higher deflection risks in the trade area for lifestyle footwear.
DSW • 0.57 mi 2.5 · Low
2026-03-01
DSW: Clearance Deals up to 60% Off Select Women's Styles Active March 2026
Clearance deals up to 60% off select women's styles from Mix No. 6, Crocs, adidas, Steve Madden, Jessica Simpson [Discoup]
The product overlap with Nike's core assortment is relatively low, limiting direct deflection risk. However, DSW's broad footwear selection and convenience-focused value messaging may attract shoppers who are deciding between a Nike visit and a multi-brand footwear trip.
JD Sports • 0.91 mi 2.5 · Low
2026-03-27
JD Sports: Ongoing Up to 50% Off Select Styles Sales Events
Up to 50% off select styles during sales events (ongoing March 2026)
This promotion offers customers a clear and easy way to save, with up to half off select styles and no complicated steps to redeem. Because it happens regularly and is available both online and in-store, shoppers are more likely to take advantage of it. Plus, since the store is close by, customers find it convenient to visit, which helps boost sales during the promo period.

Store Context
Nike Williamsburg is a Nike retail location in Brooklyn, NYC — serving a diverse mix of performance athletes, streetwear enthusiasts, and casual shoppers. The store benefits from Nike's premium positioning and strong brand loyalty within its local trade area.
★ Top Event
LES Little League Opening Day Parade
2026-04-04 • Parade starts at Grand Street Settlement • 1.1 mi from store • Parade
Impact: 8/10 High Incrementality
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The parade has a very high impact on foot traffic because it can attract between 175 and 375 attendees near the store, which is a significant portion of the venue's 500-person capacity; despite being 1.1 miles away, the weekend morning timing supports strong turnout.
NYCRUNS Brooklyn Experience Half Marathon
2026-04-26 • Greenpoint Terminal Warehouse → Prospect Park • 0.8 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The NYCRUNS Brooklyn Experience Half Marathon draws over 25,000 runners through North Brooklyn, with the race expo and start area at Greenpoint Terminal Warehouse on West Street — approximately 0.8 miles from the store. The course routes along the East River waterfront and through Williamsburg before heading south toward Prospect Park, creating strong pre- and post-race foot traffic directly in the store trade area. The athletic demographic is a near-perfect match for the Nike consumer.
LES Print Fair
2026-04-25 to 2026-04-26 • Sara D. Roosevelt Park • 1.6 mi from store • Street Fair
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The LES Print Fair is expected to draw a strong crowd with an estimated attendance between 300 and 1,800 people, which is well below the venue capacity of about 5,000, indicating a high but not maximum demand impact; although the event is 1.6 miles from the store, its weekend timing starting at 11:00 supports increased foot traffic near the area.
Brooklyn Nets v. Sacramento Kings
2026-03-29 • Barclays Center • 2.6 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Brooklyn Nets vs. Sacramento Kings game at Barclays Center, with a capacity of about 17.7k and expected attendance between 7.8k and 17.4k, is a major crowd draw on a weekend evening, likely generating high foot traffic near Nike Williamsburg despite being 2.6 miles away; however, actual turnout may vary below venue capacity.

Store Context
Nike Unite - Harlem is a Nike retail location in Manhattan, NYC — serving a diverse mix of performance athletes, streetwear enthusiasts, and casual shoppers. The store benefits from Nike's premium positioning and strong brand loyalty within its local trade area.
★ Top Event
New York Yankees vs. Miami Marlins
2026-04-03 to 2026-04-05 • Yankee Stadium • 1.7 mi from store • Sports
Impact: 9/10 High Incrementality
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The event at Yankee Stadium has a very high impact on foot traffic due to its large venue capacity of about 54,000 and an expected attendance ranging from approximately 23,800 to 52,900 people, indicating a major crowd draw despite the 1.7-mile distance from the store and weekday timing; however, actual turnout may vary significantly below capacity.

2026-04-07 to 2026-04-09 • Yankee Stadium • 1.7 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The event at Yankee Stadium has a very high impact on foot traffic due to its large venue capacity of about 54,000 and an expected attendance ranging from approximately 23,800 to 52,900, indicating a major crowd draw despite being 1.7 miles from the store; the strong ticketed attendance intent and weekday evening timing further support significant demand near the area.

2026-04-07 to 2026-04-09 • Yankee Stadium • 1.7 mi from store • Sports

The New York Yankees vs. Athletics game at Yankee Stadium, with a venue capacity of about 54,000 and an expected turnout between approximately 24,000 and 53,000 attendees, is a very high impact event for foot traffic despite the store being 1.7 miles away; the large crowd draw on a weekday evening near the store significantly increases potential customer presence, though actual turnout may vary below capacity.

Juneteenth
2026-06-19 • Citywide — Federal holiday • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Juneteenth is a federal public holiday with deep cultural significance in Harlem. The holiday draws large community gatherings along 125th Street and Marcus Garvey Park, creating one of the most significant cultural demand days of the summer for the Unite store. Community programming and local celebrations elevate foot traffic materially throughout the day.

Active Promotions Detail
Super Runners Shop • 3.41 mi 3.9 · Low
2026-03-01
Super Runners Shop: Up to 70% off discount codes active through April 2026
Up to 70% off discount codes available for March 2026 (expires April 2026 for some) [Source] - https://super-runners-shop.tenereteam.com/coupons; Multiple storewide coupons offering 20%-50% off orders, valid through March and some into April 2026
This promotion offers a strong discount that's easy for customers to use, which helps encourage purchases. However, since the promoted items don’t closely match the store’s main products, the impact on sales is limited. Store Context
Nike Clearance Store - Flushing Queens is a Nike retail location in Queens, NYC — serving a diverse mix of performance athletes, streetwear enthusiasts, and casual shoppers. The store benefits from Nike's premium positioning and strong brand loyalty within its local trade area.
★ Top Event
New York Mets Baseball Season
2026-04-01 to 2026-09-27 • Citi Field • 0.6 mi from store • Sports
Impact: 8.8/10 High Incrementality
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The New York Mets game at nearby Citi Field, with a large venue capacity of about 45,000 and expected attendance between 18,000 and 40,500, will generate very high foot traffic demand for the Nike Clearance Store due to its close proximity of 0.6 miles, despite the uncertainty that actual turnout may be lower than full capacity.
Governors Ball Music Festival
2026-06-05 to 2026-06-07 • Flushing Meadows–Corona Park • 1.3 mi from store • Festival
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Governors Ball 2026 returns to Flushing Meadows–Corona Park for three days of live music headlined by Lorde, A$AP Rocky, and Stray Kids, drawing over 60,000 attendees daily. The festival is directly accessible via the 7 train which stops near the store, and the demographic — 16 to 35 year-old urban music and streetwear consumers — is one of the strongest alignments with the Nike customer in the portfolio. This is the highest-impact June event for this store.
Mets Home Opener
2026-03-26 • Citi Field — Main Gate → Roosevelt Ave / Flushing • 0.2 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Mets Home Opener at Citi Field is expected to draw a very high turnout of approximately 16,800 to 37,800 attendees, which is close to the venue's 42,000 capacity, generating significant foot traffic near the Nike Clearance Store located just 0.2 miles away; although the event is on a weekday, the major crowd draw and proximity support a very high impact on demand.
Flushing Chinatown Street Festivals
2026-04-01 to 2026-10-31 • Main St / Flushing • 0.2 mi from store • Street Fair
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Flushing Chinatown Street Festivals are expected to draw a very high foot traffic impact due to a strong crowd signal and proximity of only 0.2 miles from the store; this large local gathering on a weekday starting at 11:00 is likely to significantly increase demand at the nearby Nike Clearance Store.

2026-04-18 to 2026-10-31 • Queens Night Market / NYSCI → 111th St / Corona • 1.1 mi from store • Street Fair
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Queens Night Market is a major crowd draw with a venue capacity of about 10,000 people, but expected attendance is likely between 600 and 3,500, which is high enough to significantly increase foot traffic near the Nike Clearance Store despite the event being 1.1 miles away and turnout potentially much lower than capacity.
New York Mets vs. Pittsburgh Pirates
2026-03-26 to 2026-03-29 • Citi Field • 1.1 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The event at Citi Field has a high impact on foot traffic due to its large venue capacity of about 41.9k and an expected attendance range between 18.4k and 41.1k, indicating a major crowd draw; although the stadium is 1.1 miles from the store and the event occurs on a weekday early afternoon, the strong attendance intent from ticketed sales supports a likely high turnout near the upper bound.
New York Mets vs. Athletics
2026-04-10 to 2026-04-12 • Citi Field • 1.1 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The New York Mets vs. Athletics game at Citi Field is expected to draw a high turnout between 18,400 and 41,100 attendees, which is close to the venue's 41,900 capacity, generating high foot traffic demand near the Nike Clearance Store despite the event being 1.1 miles away and on a weekday afternoon.

● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Queens Crossing Spring Festival is expected to draw a steady crowd of approximately 500 to 1,400 attendees, which is high relative to the venue's capacity of about 2,000 people. Its very close proximity to the Nike Clearance Store and weekend timing further support a high impact on foot traffic.

The New York Mets vs. Arizona Diamondbacks game at Citi Field, with a capacity of about 41.9k and expected attendance between 18.4k and 41.1k, is a major crowd draw likely to increase foot traffic near the Nike Clearance Store in Flushing Queens despite the venue being 1.1 miles away and the event occurring on a weekday evening.

Memorial Day weekend supports elevated retail activity in Flushing and the wider Queens area. The long weekend draws strong foot traffic to the College Point retail corridor.

Mother's Day drives strong gifting foot traffic in Flushing. The College Point retail corridor and nearby Flushing shopping areas see elevated weekend traffic from Queens families making gifting purchases.

Store Context
Nike Factory Store - Staten Island is a Nike retail location in Staten Island, NYC — serving a diverse mix of performance athletes, streetwear enthusiasts, and casual shoppers. The store benefits from Nike's premium positioning and strong brand loyalty within its local trade area.
★ Top Event
Staten Island Ferry Weekend Crowds
2026-04-01 to 2026-10-31 • St. George Ferry Terminal → Richmond Terrace / St. George • 0.1 mi from store • Community Event
Impact: 7.3/10 High Incrementality
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Staten Island Ferry Weekend Crowds event is expected to draw a high level of foot traffic near the Nike Factory Store due to its very close proximity (0.1 miles) to the St. George Ferry Terminal, which can hold up to about 15,000 people. Although the actual turnout is uncertain and may be much lower, the likely attendance range of 1,200 to 5,200 people still represents a significant influx of potential customers during the weekday morning start time.

● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Italian Cultural Festival near the Nike Factory Store has a high impact on demand due to its major crowd draw; its close proximity (0.5 miles) and midday weekday timing further support increased foot traffic, despite some uncertainty about the actual turnout.

2026-04-12 • St. George Theatre • 0.2 mi from store • Concert
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The event is expected to draw a moderate impact on foot traffic due to its close proximity to the store and weekend timing, but the likely turnout of 35 to 114 attendees is much lower than the venue's 1.9k capacity, limiting demand increase.
5K Run for Charity
2026-04-11 • St. George Waterfront • 0.1 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The 5K Run for Charity at St. George Waterfront has a venue capacity of about 200 people, with an expected turnout between 80 and 180 attendees, which is a significant number relative to the store's location just 0.1 miles away. Although the venue capacity limits maximum foot traffic, the event's weekend morning timing and close proximity to the store suggest a high potential for increased customer visits, despite some uncertainty in exact turnout.

Store Context
Nike Unite - East New York is a Nike retail location in Brooklyn, NYC — serving a diverse mix of performance athletes, streetwear enthusiasts, and casual shoppers. The store benefits from Nike's premium positioning and strong brand loyalty within its local trade area.
★ Top Event
Father's Day
2026-06-21 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
Impact: 7.5/10 High Incrementality
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Father's Day is one of the strongest gifting weekends for mall-format stores. Gateway Center draws concentrated family shopping traffic from East New York and surrounding areas, with Nike footwear being a top-performing Father's Day gift category.
Mother's Day
2026-05-10 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Mother's Day is one of the strongest gifting weekends for mall-format stores. Gateway Center draws concentrated gifting traffic from East New York and surrounding Brooklyn neighbourhoods. Nike footwear and apparel rank among the top gift categories.
Memorial Day Weekend
2026-05-23 to 2026-05-25 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Memorial Day weekend is one of the strongest retail weekends for mall-format stores. Gateway Center draws elevated family shopping traffic across the three-day weekend, with strong seasonal athletic and footwear purchasing.
Juneteenth
2026-06-19 • Citywide — Federal holiday • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Juneteenth is a federal holiday with strong community resonance in East New York and surrounding Brooklyn neighbourhoods. Community celebrations elevate foot traffic to the Gateway Center.
Good Friday
2026-04-03 • Citywide — NYC schools closed • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Good Friday school closures increase daytime foot traffic at the Gateway Center mall. The Easter long weekend is a strong retail period for mall-format stores.

Store Context
Nike - North Bronx is a Nike retail location in Bronx, NYC — serving a diverse mix of performance athletes, streetwear enthusiasts, and casual shoppers. The store benefits from Nike's premium positioning and strong brand loyalty within its local trade area.
★ Top Event
Bronx Week Festival
2026-05-17 • Grand Concourse, Bronx • 2.5 mi from store • Street Fair
Impact: 6.7/10 High Incrementality
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Bronx Week Festival is expected to draw a major crowd with attendance likely between 3,000 and 17,500 people, which is high but well below the venue's 50,000 capacity. Despite the event being 2.5 miles from the store, the weekend timing supports increased foot traffic, leading to a high impact on demand.
Bronx Week Parade
2026-05-18 • Grand Concourse, Bronx • 2.5 mi from store • Parade
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Bronx Week Parade is expected to draw a major crowd with an attendance range between approximately 1,800 and 10,500 people, which is significant though below the venue's 30,000 capacity. Despite the event being 2.5 miles from the store and occurring on a weekday, the large potential turnout suggests a high impact on foot traffic near the store.
Bronx Zoo Spring Break
2026-04-10 to 2026-04-18 • Bronx Zoo • 2.8 mi from store • Community Event
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Bronx Zoo Spring Break event is expected to draw a major crowd with attendance likely between 4,000 and 17,500 people, which is high relative to typical foot traffic; 8 miles from the store still support a high impact on demand.
Memorial Day Weekend
2026-05-23 to 2026-05-25 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Memorial Day weekend drives retail uplift in the Baychester / Co-op City area. The long weekend increases foot traffic to the Bay Plaza shopping complex adjacent to the store.
Mother's Day
2026-05-10 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Mother's Day drives retail uplift in the Baychester area. Families make gifting shopping trips to the Bay Plaza and surrounding retail, with Nike footwear being a popular gift category.
Father's Day
2026-06-21 • Citywide • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Father's Day elevates retail traffic in the Baychester area. Families make gifting shopping trips to Bay Plaza and surrounding retail, with Nike footwear and apparel among the top gift categories.
NY Botanical Garden Orchid Show
2026-04-01 to 2026-04-26 • New York Botanical Garden, 2900 Southern Blvd, Bronx • 2.3 mi from store • Cultural
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The NYBG Orchid Show draws tens of thousands of visitors to the Bronx through late April, making it one of the most significant recurring cultural draw events in the borough. Visitors travelling to the Botanical Garden from across the city often combine the trip with nearby retail stops, supporting foot traffic in the Baychester area.
Good Friday
2026-04-03 • Citywide — NYC schools closed • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Good Friday school closures drive moderate retail uplift in the Baychester area. The Easter long weekend supports above-average foot traffic at nearby shopping centres.
Bronx River Festival
2026-05-09 • Concrete Plant Park, Bronx River Greenway • 2.5 mi from store • Festival
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Bronx River Festival is expected to draw a strong crowd of approximately 750 to 2,100 attendees, which is below the venue's 3,000-person capacity, indicating moderate demand impact. Despite the event's weekend timing and strong crowd draw, its location 2.5 miles from the store likely limits foot traffic increase, resulting in a moderate impact level.
Bronx Zoo Run for the Wild
2026-04-26 • Bronx Zoo • 2.8 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Bronx Zoo Run for the Wild event is expected to draw a steady crowd of approximately 800 to 1,800 attendees, which is moderate relative to the venue's capacity of about 2,000 people. Although the event is on a weekend morning and located 2.8 miles from the store, the turnout is likely to generate moderate foot traffic impact due to the distance and uncertainty in actual attendance.
Bronx Night Market
2026-04-25 to 2026-10-24 • Fordham Plaza, Bronx • 2.5 mi from store • Market
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Bronx Night Market at Fordham Plaza can attract a strong crowd. Despite the event's weekend timing and strong crowd draw, its location 2.5 miles from the Nike - North Bronx store limits immediate foot traffic impact, resulting in a moderate demand increase.
Juneteenth
2026-06-19 • Citywide — Federal holiday • Citywide • Public Holiday
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Juneteenth is a federal holiday with community significance in the Bronx. The holiday drives elevated leisure and retail activity in the Baychester area.
Bay Plaza Spring Sidewalk Sale
2026-04-29 to 2026-04-30 • Bay Plaza Shopping Center • 0.8 mi from store • Shopping
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Bay Plaza Spring Sidewalk Sale is expected to draw a steady crowd of approximately 120 to 700 attendees, which is well below the venue capacity of about 2,000 people, indicating moderate demand impact due to weekday timing and close proximity to the store.
Fordham Rams Football - Spring Game
2026-04-25 • Coffey Field • 3 mi from store • Sports
● BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
The Fordham Rams Football Spring Game at Coffey Field has a high impact on foot traffic due to its strong crowd draw and expected attendance between 2,800 and 6,300 people, which approaches the venue's 7,000 capacity. Although the venue is about 3 miles from the store, the weekend afternoon timing supports higher turnout, making increased demand likely despite some uncertainty in actual attendance.

⚙
Methodology
1
Store Intelligence Engine
Agent: Store Intelligence Agent • Coverage: 9 Nike stores across NYC boroughs
Computes 12 aggregate scores and 5 composite indices per store using POI density, transit data, competitor mapping, demographic fit, and event amplification signals.
12 Aggregate Scores5 Composite IndicesTrade Area Profiling
2
Competitive Landscape Engine
Agent: Competitive Intelligence Agent • Coverage: Direct & indirect competitors per store
Maps and scores all sportswear, apparel, and footwear competitors within the 0.5 mi trade area.
Competition Pressure IndexDirect Competitor Threat
3
Event Intelligence Engine
Agent: Event Intelligence Agent • Coverage: 1,283 events tracked across 9 stores
AI Agents discover and score events using a 4-component model: brand alignment, scale, proximity decay, and context bonus. Scores are calibrated for artist popularity and sports matchup significance. Capacity suppressed for open-air events. Events before March 24, 2026 excluded.
1,283 Events TrackedArtist Popularity CalibrationProximity Decay Applied
4
Promo Discovery Engine
Agent: Web Intelligence Agent • Coverage: 613 promos across 9 stores
AI Agents autonomously discover and extract active promotions across competitor brands. Revenue deflection risk is computed per brand per store using EDP, W-SKU overlap, ease of redemption, recurrence, and distance decay.
613 Promos9 StoresRevenue Deflection RankedSource URLs Traced
5
Weather & Seasonal Impact Modelling
Horizon: 16-day rolling forecast • Signals: Temperature • Precipitation • Wind Speed
Each alert is scored against historical baselines with hourly anomalies layered on top. Impact direction and magnitude interpreted factoring in store hours and weekday/weekend weighting. Days with zero impact score are suppressed.
Historical BaselineHourly AnomaliesWeekday / Weekend WeightingZero-Impact Days Suppressed
ⓘ
Score Definitions
13 Dimension Scores
Competition Pressure
Overall density of competing businesses in the surrounding trade area. A higher score reflects a more saturated competitive environment, which also signals strong category demand in the location.
Direct Competitor Threat
Concentration of direct, like-for-like competitors operating nearby. A higher score indicates greater head-to-head competitive exposure within the immediate trade area.
Athletic Ecosystem
Presence of gyms, fitness studios, and sporting goods retailers in the area. Reflects a health- and activity-oriented consumer base that generates consistent, routine-driven foot traffic.
Retail Culture Density
Density of apparel, specialty, and lifestyle retail in the trade area. A strong score indicates a shopping-oriented neighbourhood with high discretionary trip frequency throughout the day.
Event Amplification
Volume and relevance of nearby events. Captures the degree to which local events — festivals, concerts, sports, community gatherings — create incremental foot traffic opportunities for businesses in the area.
Transit Connectivity
Accessibility of public transit stops within half a mile, including subway, bus, and ferry. Strong scores reflect locations with broad catchment reach and sustained pedestrian throughput driven by commuter and visitor flows.
Tourism
Presence of hotels, landmarks, and tourist attractions in the trade area. Higher scores indicate locations that draw visitor spend alongside resident demand, broadening the potential customer base.
Place Density
Total volume of points of interest within the trade area, serving as a broad proxy for urban density and neighbourhood activity. The strongest single indicator of how commercially active a location is day-to-day.
Dining Density
Concentration of food and beverage establishments in the trade area. A high score signals a strong eating-out culture and robust foot traffic during meal periods.
Nightlife Culture
Presence of bars, entertainment venues, and late-night businesses nearby. Reflects the area's potential for extended-hour demand and evening foot traffic beyond standard retail and dining hours.
Anchor Ecosystem
Proximity to major retail anchors such as department stores, malls, and flagship destinations. Anchors generate durable, multi-purpose visitor traffic that benefits nearby businesses throughout the day.
Demographic Fit
How well the surrounding population profile aligns with the brand's target customer. Based on census-level indicators including income, density, and education.
Walkability
Quality of pedestrian infrastructure around the location, derived from street-level walk networks. Higher scores indicate lower friction for spontaneous visits and stronger conversion from passing foot traffic.
13 dimension scores are aggregated into the 5 composite indices shown opposite.
5 Composite Indices
Overall Store Strength
The headline measure of a location's trade area quality across all positive dimensions, adjusted for competitive pressure. Used to rank and compare locations within a portfolio.
Net Opportunity
The estimated upside available at a location after accounting for competitive gaps and local demand dynamics. A location can score lower on overall strength but higher on net opportunity where demand is underserved relative to competition.
Trade Area Vibrancy
A measure of how active and commercially energised the surrounding area is, combining dining, nightlife, retail culture, events, and tourism signals. High vibrancy indicates an area that generates its own foot traffic independent of a single destination.
Accessibility Score
How easily customers can reach and discover the location, driven by transit access, walkability, and surrounding place density. Stronger scores are associated with higher visit frequency and broader catchment draw.
Event Revenue Potential
The degree to which nearby events, amplified by tourism exposure and trade area vibrancy, create incremental revenue opportunities. Identifies locations best positioned to benefit from event-driven traffic spikes and targeted activations.

Nike NYC Retail Intelligence Report • Powered by Propheus Retail Agent • March 2026
